body.label: source.root.thm
body.claim: Let $A$ be an $n \times n$ matrix with entries in $\mathbb{R}$. If $A^2 = -I_n$, where $I_n$ is the $n \times n$ identity matrix, then $n$ is an even integer.

proof_steps.claim: Let $A$ be an $n \times n$ matrix with entries in $\mathbb{R}$. If $A^2 = -I_n$, where $I_n$ is the $n \times n$ identity matrix, then $n$ is an even integer.
